title: Articles (a / an / the / zero)
subtitle: The tiniest words examiners count the loudest
intro: Article misuse is the single most common fossilized error in essays from speakers of languages that lack articles (Turkish, Russian, Mandarin, Japanese, Vietnamese). A Band 6.0 essay often has ten article errors; a Band 7.5 essay has one or two. Articles are invisible when correct and glaring when wrong — mastering them is a disproportionate investment in your score.

rules.heading: Indefinite (a/an) = one unspecified member of a class
rules.body: Use for the first mention of a countable singular noun: 'A student submitted an essay'. 'An' before a vowel sound, not letter: 'an hour', 'a university'.

rules.heading: Definite (the) = the listener knows which one
rules.body: Second mention, unique entities ('the sun', 'the government'), superlatives ('the best'), ordinals ('the first'), and post-modified nouns ('the policy that was introduced in 2019').

rules.heading: Zero article = generalisation or uncountable
rules.body: Plurals for general reference ('Students need support'), uncountables ('Education is...'), most country names, meals, and abstract concepts ('Freedom, equality, democracy').

rules.heading: Institutions — function vs building
rules.body: 'Go to school / university / hospital' (as a student/patient — zero article) vs. 'go to the school / the hospital' (as a visitor). A small but reliable Band 6.5+ signal.

examples.sentence: Education is the foundation of a prosperous society.
examples.note: Zero article for general concept; 'the' with superlative-style noun phrase.

examples.sentence: A student who attends a well-funded school has a measurable advantage over one who does not.
examples.note: First-mention 'a student' and 'a school'; the comparison is generic.

examples.sentence: The chart illustrates the proportion of households using renewable energy.
examples.note: 'The chart' — the one in front of us; 'the proportion' — specific to this chart.

examples.sentence: My grandmother was the kindest person I have ever met.
The Internet, the smartphone and the electric car have all reshaped modern life.
examples.note: Generic-defining use of 'the' with class-representative nouns.

band8_marker: Band 8 writers show mastery in the hard cases: they know when 'education' is uncountable-general (no article) versus 'the education system' (definite, specific entity). They produce idiomatic institutional phrases ('went to hospital' vs. 'went to the hospital') and handle noun phrases with post-modifiers ('the policies introduced in 2019', not 'policies that introduced').

common_errors.fix: Singular countable needs an article → 'I am a university student'.
'Era' is countable → 'Life in the modern era'.

practice.title: Article audit
practice.instruction: Click the article error in each sentence. If a word is missing, click the spot where it should go.

items.prompt: [A:The education] is key to [B:a] stable democracy.
items.explanation: General concept → zero article: 'Education'.

items.prompt: She is [A:a] most influential scientist [B:of the] twenty-first century.
items.explanation: Superlative takes 'the' → 'the most'.

items.prompt: Pollution threatens [A:a public health] and damages [B:the natural environment].
items.explanation: 'Public health' is an uncountable, general concept → zero article: 'threatens public health'. 'A public health' is ungrammatical.

items.prompt: [A:The government] should invest more in [B:public transportation].
items.explanation: No error — both articles are correct.

items.prompt: I go to [A:the gym] three times a week and to [B:work] by bicycle.
items.explanation: Both idiomatic: 'the gym' (the specific one I use), 'to work' (function).

items.prompt: He is reading [A:a book] that [B:a author] recommended.
items.explanation: 'Author' starts with vowel sound → 'an author'.

items.prompt: [A:Life] in [B:twenty-first century] is faster than ever.
items.explanation: Centuries take 'the' → 'the twenty-first century'.

items.prompt: She was admitted to [A:hospital] after [B:an] accident.
items.explanation: 'Admitted to hospital' is idiomatic British zero-article; 'an accident' correct.

practice.title: Fill the article (or leave blank for zero)
practice.instruction: Type a, an, the, or — for zero article.

items.prompt: ___ poverty remains ___ greatest challenge of our time.
items.explanation: General abstract noun + superlative.

items.prompt: She works as ___ engineer at ___ company that develops electric vehicles.
items.explanation: First mention, countable singular.

items.prompt: ___ sun rises in ___ east.
items.explanation: Unique entities.

items.prompt: Many students rely on ___ public transport to get to ___ university.
items.explanation: General reference + institutional function.

items.prompt: ___ Amazon is ___ longest river in South America.
items.explanation: Rivers always take 'the'; superlatives take 'the'.

items.prompt: ___ honest answer is sometimes more valuable than ___ clever one.
items.explanation: 'Honest' starts with a vowel sound (silent h).

items.prompt: Governments must address ___ climate change before it reaches ___ irreversible tipping point.
items.explanation: General issue + first mention of a concept.

items.prompt: I spent ___ year studying in ___ UK before returning home.
items.explanation: Duration with 'a'; 'the UK' is a defined entity.

practice.title: Article decisions
practice.instruction: Pick the version that sounds most natural to an examiner.

items.prompt: ___ rapid urbanisation has strained infrastructure worldwide.
items.explanation: Definite because the noun is specified by an adjective + post-modifier context.

items.prompt: Online education has become ___ viable alternative for millions.
items.explanation: One of many alternatives → 'a viable alternative'.

items.prompt: ___ elderly often face social isolation.
items.explanation: 'The + adjective' refers to a group: 'the elderly', 'the rich'.

items.prompt: She was awarded ___ Nobel Prize in literature.
items.explanation: Unique prize name takes 'the'.

items.prompt: He is studying ___ law at ___ Oxford.
items.explanation: Field of study and named university take zero article.

items.prompt: ___ most pressing issue facing humanity is ___ climate breakdown.
items.explanation: Superlative 'the'; abstract phenomenon zero article.

items.prompt: In ___ 1990s, ___ internet was still a novelty.
items.explanation: Decades take 'the'; 'the internet' as a unique entity.

items.prompt: ___ Japanese have one of the highest life expectancies in the world.
items.explanation: 'The + nationality adjective' = the people of that country.
